title: What Art School Didn't Teach Me

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>Art school taught me technique. <break time="350ms"/>It didn't teach me how to survive being an artist.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>Four years at RISD. <break time="300ms"/>Color theory. <break time="250ms"/>Composition. <break time="200ms"/>Art history. <break time="400ms"/>Not one class on imposter syndrome. <break time="350ms"/>Not one lecture on what to do when the voice in your head says everything you make is garbage. <break time="500ms"/>I graduated thinking I was ready. <break time="400ms"/>I wasn't ready for the silence after school ends. <break time="350ms"/>The lack of deadlines. <break time="300ms"/>The self-direction required. <break time="250ms"/>The endless comparison.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>The hardest skills - <break time="400ms"/>creating despite doubt, finishing despite fear, surviving comparison - <break time="350ms"/>nobody taught me those. <break time="500ms"/>If you went to school for this and still struggle: <break time="400ms"/>that's not your failure. <break time="350ms"/>That's a gap in the education. <break time="300ms"/>You were taught to make things. <break time="250ms"/>Not to survive making things.

when_to_share: When someone with creative training feels they should know better
